info.title: nulink
info.description: The missing LINK feature of NuGet: develop & test packages as if they were part of the main project

commands.name: link
commands.description: Link package to a directory in local file system, machine-wide

options.name: --consumer
options.description: Path to consumer .csproj or .sln (default: first .csproj or .sln in current directory)

options.name: --package
options.description: Package id (default: all packages in consumer project/solution)

options.name: --local
options.description: Full path to package .csproj in local file system

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: If specified, list intended actions without executing them

commands.name: status
commands.description: Check status of packages in consumer project/solution

options.name: --consumer
options.description: Path to consumer .csproj or .sln (default: first .csproj or .sln in current directory)

options.name: --package
options.description: Package id (default: all packages in consumer project/solution)

options.name: --quiet
options.description: If specified, suppresses all output except data (useful for scripting)

commands.name: unlink
commands.description: Unlink package from local file system, machine-wide

options.name: --consumer
options.description: Path to consumer .csproj or .sln (default: first .csproj or .sln in current directory)

options.name: --package
options.description: Package id (default: all packages in consumer project/solution)

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: If specified, list intended actions without executing them
